props.message:
SPRAWL LIBRARY

A quieter wing of the Sprawl, where the merchants share the tools and the secrets. Quieter than the market. Just as busy.

The threads here run all the way back to the Loomhouse on the Crown.

Borrower's notice:

'A thread borrowed here is a thread the Loomhouse will eventually want back. The Sprawl pays its debts in the next breath, or the breath after — but it pays them.'

root.speaker: Bind Halor
root.text:
*she's leaning on a workbench, passing a chisel handle-first to a Local who looks faintly suspicious about the whole transaction.*

Walker. We share, here. The market's just up the way. The Library's just over my shoulder. The Sprawl runs on borrowing — and we keep good books.

loc.speaker: Bind Halor
loc.text:
She thinks I'm going to charge her for it. I'm not. I borrowed the chisel from the Loomhouse last spring; I'm not about to put a price on it.

When the Sprawl forgets that, the Sprawl breaks down. By the Source, we know it.

bye.speaker: Bind Halor
bye.text: Walk well, walker. Borrow what you need. The library is open.

root.speaker: Local Vesh
root.text:
*she's holding the chisel like it might still ask her for pebbles.*

Most Binds don't lend, walker. Halor's an exception. Most Binds want something for everything.

The Library — this Library, here — is the part of the Sprawl I trust.

bye.speaker: Local Vesh
bye.text: Walk well. And keep your pebbles in your pocket here.

root.speaker: Nix
root.text:
*she does not blink when you arrive. she has been here long enough that arrival is uninteresting*

I never change. When the realm needs me changed, it replaces me. The one before me was also called Nix. So will the one after.

who.speaker: Nix
who.text: Whichever Nix is current. The realm will know.

props.message:
Pinned by a tangle of threads at the loomhouse:

    yarn install

Wait twelve minutes.
Do not interrupt.
It is doing what it has always done.

Pinned over a workbench:

    There are two hard things in this realm:
        naming,
        cache invalidation,
        and off-by-one errors.
